description: Evaluate the central claims of a biographic-historical essay about Svetlana Alliluyeva. Claims under review: (1) her testimony functions as a counter-archive that reveals aspects of power and terror state documents structurally miss; (2) her life supports a structural, not merely personal, indictment of Soviet totalitarianism; (3) her biography is evidence that neither Soviet nor Western symbolic systems fully absorbed the human person into a usable script; (4) the essay's strongest claims are interpretive and jurisdictional rather than narrowly documentary. Assess provenance, independence, overclaim risk, and whether the claims should be treated as supported, partially supported, or overstated.

notes: Local artifact inspected directly in this environment.
